philippines.title: Ejercito welcomes PH's safe Hormuz transit pact with Iran
philippines.description:
MANILA, Philippines – Deputy Majority Leader Senator JV Ejercito welcomed the move of the Marcos administration to secure assurances for the safe passage of Philippine-flagged vessels and Filipino seafarers through the Strait of Hormuz. He said the move will facilitate the delivery ​of critical oil and fertilizer supplies to the country.    “This is a very welcome development! This will greatly help replenish our petroleum reserves. We recognize the efforts of President Marcos and the Department of Foreign Affairs in opening diplomatic channels on this matte,.” Ejercito said.  “We are hopeful it will materialize and will be sustained,” Ejercito added.  […]...
Keep on reading: Ejercito welcomes PH's safe Hormuz transit pact with Iran

philippines.title: 'Paldo' for Cebu informants: They get ₱1.6M for drug bust tips
philippines.description:
Seven civilian informants in Cebu received a total of ₱1,696,349.59 in cash rewards from the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA) for their role in operations conducted in 2025 that led to confiscation of large volumes of shabu and arrest of drug suspects....
Keep on reading: 'Paldo' for Cebu informants: They get ₱1.6M for drug bust tips

philippines.title: Mandaue eyes e-trikes as fuel costs hit drivers
philippines.description:
MANDAUE CITY, Cebu — The Mandaue City Council has approved a resolution calling for a study on the possible introduction of electric tricycles (e-trikes) for public transport, as rising fuel prices continue to impact drivers and operators. Councilor Carlo Fortuna, who authored the resolution, said the proposal aims to provide an alternative for tricycle operators struggling with increasing operating costs. He noted that current laws still require fuel-powered tricycles, limiting options for drivers who may want to explore more sustainable and cost-efficient alternatives. “Atoa tagaan og alternative ang atoang drivers and operators. Sa resolution atoang gi-urge ang TEAM and the […]...
Keep on reading: Mandaue eyes e-trikes as fuel costs hit drivers
